QF.id: XIII Trading, Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_3

question.text: What margining system is used by Indian clearing corporations for real-time computation of initial margins in derivatives?

answer.text: SPAN (Standard Portfolio Analysis of Risk) — a scenario-based approach that generates 16 what-if scenarios to determine the worst-case loss
Value at Risk (VaR) at 95% confidence level applied uniformly to all positions
A fixed percentage margin applied to all derivative contracts regardless of risk
Black-Scholes model-based margining that uses option Greeks for margin calculation

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Indian clearing corporations use SPAN®, developed by CME. SPAN takes an integrated view of the portfolio, generates 16 scenarios of price and volatility changes over a 2-day horizon, and uses the highest loss scenario as the margin requirement. This portfolio-based approach is more capital-efficient than individual position margining. **The Trap:** VaR is a component of SPAN, not a separate system. Fixed percentage margins are too crude for derivatives. Black-Scholes is for pricing, not margining. **Cross-Exam Context:** SPAN is used across ALL derivative segments in India — currency (I Ch7), interest rate (IV Ch7), equity (VIII Ch7), and commodity (XVI Ch7). Series XIII tests this unified concept. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** SPAN gives portfolio margin benefit — offsetting positions in the same underlying reduce the margin requirement compared to summing individual position margins.
